excerpt:
<details open>

Support `-fa auto` in llama-bench (#23714)

* Support `-fa auto` in llama-bench

Make the default value of `-ngl` -1, similar to other tools.

Update README with latest usage and examples

* Address review comments

</details>

**macOS/iOS:**
- [macOS Apple Silicon (arm64)](https://github.com/ggml-org/llama.cpp/releases/download/b9437/llama-b9437-bin-macos-arm64.tar.gz)
- macOS Apple Silicon (arm64, KleidiAI enabled) [DISABLED](https://github.com/ggml-org/llama.cpp/pull/23780)
- [macOS Intel (x64)](https://github.com/ggml-org/llama.cpp/releases/download/b9437/llama-b9437-bin-macos-x64.tar.gz)
- [iOS XCFramework](https://github.com/ggml-org/llama.cpp/releases/download/b9437/llama-b9437-xcframework.zip)

**Linux:**
- [Ubuntu x64 (CPU)](https://github.com/ggml-org/llama.cpp/releases/download/b9437/llama-b9437-bin-ubuntu-x64.tar.gz)
- [Ubuntu arm64 (CPU)](https://github.com/ggml-org/llama.cpp/releases/download/b9437/llama-b9437-bin-ubuntu-arm64.tar.gz)
- [Ubuntu s390x (CPU)](https://github.com/ggml-org/llama.cpp/releases/download/b9437/llama-b9437-bin-ubuntu-s390x.tar.gz)
- [Ubuntu x64 (Vulkan)](https://github.com/ggml-org/llama.cpp/releases/download/b9437/llama-b9437-b
